_meta.purpose: Hand-crafted synthetic NER+topic gold set for the Claude extractor. Mirrors the production distribution (finance, tech, personal saves, founder/investing content, philosophical notes). NO real user data — every name, company, and quote is fabricated or sourced from public-domain reference (well-known people/products).
_meta.scoring: Set-based P/R/F1 with case-insensitive + intra-space-collapsed entity match (so 'OpenAI' == 'Open AI'). Topics: exact lowercase match after strip. See connecting_dots/enrichment/judge.py.

items.name: naval_wealth_tweet
items.title: Naval Ravikant on wealth vs status
items.text: Seek wealth, not money or status. Wealth is having assets that earn while you sleep. Money is how we transfer time and wealth. Status is your place in the social hierarchy. Play long-term games with long-term people.

items.name: coreweave_lambda_gpu
items.title: Coreweave vs Lambda — GPU compute economics
items.text: Coreweave has H100 capacity locked in with NVIDIA. Lambda Labs going more retail. Both burning cash to lock supply before the LLM training squeeze. Microsoft and OpenAI both committed multi-year contracts.

items.name: buffett_munger_method
items.title: Warren Buffett and Charlie Munger investment method
items.text: The Berkshire approach: find a wonderful business at a fair price, not a fair business at a wonderful price. Munger called it 'sit on your ass investing'. Coca-Cola, See's Candies, Apple — concentrated bets, decades of holding.

items.name: kahneman_system_1_2
items.title: Daniel Kahneman — Thinking Fast and Slow
items.text: System 1 is fast, intuitive, emotional. System 2 is slow, deliberate, logical. Most of our decisions run on System 1 and we confabulate System 2 justifications after the fact. The anchoring effect, availability heuristic, loss aversion — all System 1 shortcuts.

items.name: yc_startup_school_advice
items.title: Y Combinator startup school — talk to users
items.text: Paul Graham's advice never changes: do things that don't scale. Talk to users. Build something people want. Sam Altman added: pick the right founder, pick the right idea, build a great product, get to PMF. Everything else is noise.

items.name: spaced_repetition_anki
items.title: Spaced repetition for technical interviews
items.text: Using Anki for system design and DSA. The SM-2 algorithm spreads reviews exponentially. Michael Nielsen's essay on Augmenting Long-term Memory convinced me. Daily 20-card reviews, no skipping.

items.name: second_brain_obsidian
items.title: Second brain in Obsidian — graph view
items.text: Tiago Forte's PARA framework mapped onto Obsidian. The graph view actually shows clusters when wikilinks are dense enough. Roam was first but Obsidian won on local-first and plugins.

items.name: fed_rate_cut_2026
items.title: Fed rate cut and the bond market
items.text: Jerome Powell signaled a 25bp cut at the FOMC meeting. Ten-year treasury yields dropped 12bp. Mortgage spreads still elevated. Inflation print last week was 2.4% headline, 2.7% core. Soft landing scenario still in play.

items.name: india_household_brands
items.title: India household brand investment thesis
items.text: HUL, Nestle India, Britannia — the consumption story. Patanjali disruption mostly digested. D-Mart still the operating story. Tier-2 city penetration the next leg. Rural distribution is the moat.

items.name: claude_anthropic_release
items.title: Anthropic ships Claude 4.7
items.text: Adaptive thinking only — no more budget_tokens. 1M context window standard pricing. New high-resolution vision up to 2576px. The xhigh effort level is the new default in Claude Code.

items.name: rick_astley_song
items.title: Rick Astley — Never Gonna Give You Up
items.text: Released 1987. Stock Aitken Waterman production. The original music video has 1.5 billion YouTube views. Rickrolling as a meme started around 2007 on 4chan.

items.name: vikas_khanna_recipe
items.title: Bhapa Doi by chef Vikas Khanna
items.text: Bengali steamed yogurt dessert. Hung curd, condensed milk, cardamom. Steam 20 minutes in ramekins, chill overnight. Garnish with pistachios. The chef demonstrated this on a YouTube short.

items.name: akash_decentralized_compute
items.title: Akash Network — decentralized GPU compute
items.text: Akash is a marketplace for compute on Cosmos. Anyone can list a GPU rig and earn AKT. Pricing 50-70% below AWS for equivalent A100/H100 instances. The bet: AI compute demand outpaces hyperscaler buildout.

items.name: philosophy_consciousness
items.title: On consciousness and the hard problem
items.text: David Chalmers framed the hard problem of consciousness in 1995. Why is there something it is like to be a thing? Daniel Dennett pushes back: it's an illusion all the way down. Integrated Information Theory (Tononi) gets specific.

items.name: shoe_dog_phil_knight
items.title: Shoe Dog by Phil Knight
items.text: Phil Knight's memoir on building Nike. Started selling Onitsuka Tigers out of his car after the Stanford MBA. The break with Bowerman, the Japanese trips, Blue Ribbon Sports becoming Nike in 1971. Worth re-reading every few years.

items.name: whatsapp_image_no_caption
items.title: WhatsApp Image 42

items.name: plain_thought_no_entities
items.title: A plain text thought
items.text: Sometimes you just need to write something down to figure out what you actually think. The medium is the message and the writing is the thinking.

items.name: thiel_zero_to_one
items.title: Thiel — Zero to One
items.text: Peter Thiel's thesis: competition is for losers. Build a monopoly. The PayPal mafia did exactly that. Last mover advantage > first mover. Definite optimism vs indefinite optimism.

items.name: mit_algorithms_lecture
items.title: MIT 6.006 — Dynamic Arrays
items.text: Erik Demaine's lecture on dynamic arrays. Amortized O(1) append via doubling. The python list internals do exactly this. Resizing factor 1.125 vs the textbook 2x because Python optimizes for memory.

items.name: mutual_funds_india_sip
items.title: Mutual fund SIPs in India — 5 year returns
items.text: Parag Parikh Flexi Cap topping the charts at 24% CAGR over 5 years. Mirae Asset Large Cap solid 17%. SBI Small Cap volatile but 26% if you stayed in. Nifty 50 index funds boring 14%.

items.name: byju_collapse
items.title: Lessons from Byju's collapse
items.text: Aggressive expansion through Whitehat Jr, Aakash, Epic acquisitions. Burned through $5B in funding. Sequoia and Prosus marked it down to near zero. The aggressive sales culture and accounting issues caught up. Edtech valuation reset for everyone.

items.name: ai_evaluation_playbook
items.title: AI evaluation playbook — Hamel Husain
items.text: Hamel's three pillars: error analysis first, then write the judge prompt, then validate the judge against human labels. Don't trust LLM-as-judge until you've calibrated it. Track precision/recall per failure mode.

items.name: self_dm_random_url
items.title: https://example.com/smoke-url
items.text: Smoke test. No content.

items.name: stocks_to_watch_2026
items.title: Stocks to watch — Indian Hume Pipe, Ashok Leyland
items.text: Indian Hume Pipe Company on a multi-year capex cycle. Ashok Leyland medium commercial vehicle pickup. Both classic infrastructure plays. Looking at PE compression vs forward earnings.

items.name: ai_pmf_startup
items.title: AI startups in upskilling — talent assessment infrastructure
items.text: Sybill, Korn Ferry exploring AI assessments. Mercer pushing skills graph. Going Out economy thesis for India — Anysphere doing developer hiring at scale. Building behavioural assessment evals is the moat.

items.name: buffett_munger_research_method
items.title: Warren Buffett Charlie Munger deep analysis method
items.text: Read 500 pages a day. Mental models from multiple disciplines — biology, physics, psychology, history. Munger's lattice of theory. Buffett's owner earnings calculation. Look-through earnings for equity portfolios.

items.name: perplexity_search_history
items.title: Perplexity and the future of search
items.text: Aravind Srinivas building a Google alternative on top of LLMs. Citations as first-class output. The Pro Search agentic flow. Free tier ad-supported, Pro at $20/month. Competing with Google's AI Overviews and ChatGPT search.

items.name: weekly_protocol_health
items.title: Dhiraj daily protocol
items.text: Wake 6am. 20 min Wim Hof breathing. Cold shower. 30g protein breakfast. Vitamin D 2000IU. B12 sublingual. Walk 8000 steps. No screens after 10pm.

items.name: newton_systematic_approach
items.title: Newton would approach this systematically
items.text: Isaac Newton's method: observe, hypothesize, test, refine. The Principia Mathematica laid out the calculus and the laws of motion simultaneously. Standing on the shoulders of giants — Galileo, Kepler, Hooke. The Royal Society as the institutional layer.

items.name: happiness_rankings_data
items.title: World happiness rankings analysis
items.text: Nordic countries dominate — Finland, Denmark, Iceland, Norway, Sweden in the top 7. Strong correlation with social trust, low corruption, high welfare spending. The US ranks 23rd, slipping. India around 126. The Gallup World Poll methodology debated.
